title: Elvetham Liberator Memorial
memorial_type: Graves at Brookwood Cemetery; Czechoslovak section memorial

maintainer.notes: Graves at Brookwood Cemetery maintained by CWGC.

external_narratives.summary: On 5 October 1945, Consolidated Liberator GR.VI KG867 of 311 (Czechoslovak) Squadron crashed at Elvetham, Hampshire, five minutes into a repatriation flight from RAF Blackbushe to Prague. An engine fire and fuel starvation caused the crash, killing all 23 aboard.
external_narratives.key_details: All 23 victims were Czechoslovak nationals: 5 crew, 17 official passengers, and 1 stowaway. This was the largest single-loss accident involving Free Czechoslovaks in the RAF. After the crash, repatriation switched from air to surface transport.

external_narratives.summary: Details of the Czechoslovak section at Brookwood Military Cemetery where five aircrew from the Elvetham crash are buried. Thirteen passengers share a common grave in the Old Roman Catholic Ground of the civilian cemetery.
external_narratives.key_details: Burials took place on 11 October 1945. Five aircrew in the Czechoslovak military section; 13 passengers in a communal grave in Long Avenue, Old Roman Catholic Ground.

external_narratives.summary: Memorial entry for the Elvetham Air Disaster on Find a Grave, documenting the memorial at Brookwood Cemetery for the 23 Czechoslovak personnel killed on 5 October 1945.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial located at Brookwood Cemetery, Surrey. Lists all 23 victims.
